items.rationale: Believes crypto networks are the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies and the path to make 'atoms look more like bits'; views it as the future of consumer crypto and user-generated physical services. His single most repeated, evolving thesis.
items.age_note: Most consistent theme across the entire corpus (2024-06 → 2025-06); current and intensifying, not stale.

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.
1. Telecom 2. Energy 3. Transportation 4. AI 5. Healthcare 6. Robotics

items.rationale: Sees energy as one of the few industries that not only wants but needs decentralization; led Daylight's Series A to coordinate DERs and virtual power plants against monopoly utilities.
items.age_note: Heavily developed mid-2024; reaffirmed June 2025 ('Crowdsourcing solar panels and batteries is more efficient onchain'). Current.

citations.quote: Energy is one of the few non-crypto industries that doesn't just want decentralization, but needs it
Energy is a collection of highly regulated monopolies in the US today, and one we believe is ripe for disruption.

items.rationale: Now frames privacy as a key bottleneck and product problem enabled by institutional adoption; co-led Miden's seed for client-side proving with flexible privacy. TEEs and coSNARKs seen as enabling private shared state.
items.age_note: Earlier TEE/coSNARK interest (2024) escalated to a strong, explicit conviction by April 2025 — sharpened, not reversed.

citations.quote: Privacy is essential, and I'm surprised to see this take coming from the OG collaborative proofs founder.
This all sets up privacy as a key bottleneck and important product problem that wasn't the case years ago.
Enclaves (aka TEEs) are underrated.

items.rationale: Considers ZK underhyped — a new developer primitive enabling trustless off-chain compute, redundant proof verification, and decentralized proving networks (scheduling, sybil resistance, aggregation) he calls essential for blockchains.
items.age_note: Peak thread activity 2024; carried into 2025 via verifiable-AI framing. Persisted and current.

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped.
I believe proving networks will become essential for blockchains

items.rationale: Excited by AI inference as a chain primitive (Ambient useful-PoW L1) and by self-sovereign, locally-run agents as 'self custody' for AI; sees user-owned, censorship-resistant AI companions as the best verifiable-AI use case.
items.age_note: Built 2024 (PIN AI, decentralized AI); most recent and concrete signal is the June 2025 Ambient investment.

citations.quote: support their mission of building machine intelligence as a currency.
What's the best use case for verifiable AI? I believe it's user owned and operated companions.
Cloud hosted agents == not your keys, not your crypto

items.rationale: Calls seamless interoperability his 'holy grail' for rollups; backs real-time proofs (T1), based rollups (Spire), shared sequencing and proof aggregation as the coordination layer to unify a fragmented Ethereum.

citations.quote: Seamless interoperability is my holy grail for rollups, and T1 is working to finally make it possible.
One small step for Syndicate, one big step for interoperability on Ethereum

items.rationale: Sees agent-driven, per-view micropayments displacing ad/subscription models, and points to all-time-high stablecoin volume as evidence crypto is delivering real rails.
items.age_note: Listed among favorites since 2024-07; sharpened into a specific micropayments call June 2025. Current.

citations.quote: Micropayments will make a comeback
all time high stablecoin volume (higher than visa or mastercard)

items.rationale: Argues Ethereum must use non-native DA (EigenDA, Celestia, Espresso) to scale its rollup roadmap toward millions of TPS well before Danksharding arrives.
items.age_note: Detailed June 2024 thread; not restated in 2025 — persisted, somewhat dated but never reversed.

citations.quote: Ethereum will need non-native data availability to properly scale its rollup-centric roadmap

items.rationale: Surprised more teams haven't built DeFi using ZK coprocessors for complex pricing functions, historical state proofs and better risk management at lower gas.
items.age_note: Concentrated in June 2024; not restated since — persisted.

citations.quote: I think DeFi protocols should adopt risk modeling in ZK and use more complex pricing functions that run off chain and are proven trustlessly onchain

items.rationale: Thinks insurance is an obviously good idea for crypto that has never worked, but believes improved capital rehypothecation may have recently changed that — actively soliciting the best protocols.
items.age_note: Exploratory/curious stance, Jan→May 2025; current but unresolved (questions, not conviction).

citations.quote: Insurance seems like an obviously good idea for crypto. Why has it never worked?
What are the most compelling crypto insurance protocols?

items.rationale: Tokens and subsidies solve the cold-start/supply side but can't make users love a product; finding genuine PMF is the unresolved hard problem and his chief worry within his favorite sector.
items.age_note: Stated mid-2024, echoed June 2025 ('vibes not value'). Current and persistent.

citations.quote: The big hairy question for DePIN projects is finding PMF
it's hard to pay users to love a product

items.rationale: Warns against products that are 'vibes not value' and prizes ruthless engineering execution over memeing narratives; dismisses status/hype as a substitute for real product.
items.age_note: Theme from 2024 ('tokenomics is not protocol design'); sharpest statement June 2025. Current.

citations.quote: If the product is vibes and not value, find a different product. Or realize you're selling religion and entertainment
tired: memeing a narrative into the zeitgeist; wired: ruthlessly executing on your engineering roadmap

items.rationale: Worries fragmented liquidity and fractured state across L2s is a structural weakness, and that rollups may fail to ship interoperability before vertically-integrated L1s bootstrap liquidity.
items.age_note: 2024 → early 2025; framed as both challenge and opportunity. Current.

citations.quote: Can Ethereum rollups ship seamless interoperability to stop liquidity fragmentation before the vertically integrated L1s bootstrap enough liquidity?
fragmented liquidity and fractured state present a challenge and an opportunity

items.rationale: Acknowledges non-native DA — which he otherwise champions — introduces additional security assumptions beyond Ethereum's.
items.age_note: June 2024 caveat, not revisited. Persisted; secondary to his pro-DA enthusiasm.

citations.quote: Non-native DA comes with security trade offs, but that's a topic for another thread.

items.rationale: Frames the bigger risk as builders giving up too early; argues caution is fine but cynicism is self-defeating given the long timelines to replace the financial system.
items.age_note: April 2025 — a recent, current rebuttal to bearish sentiment.

citations.quote: Caution is good, but cynicism is self defeating.
Did you really think it would be easier to replace the financial system than to build an online grocery store?

items.rationale: Restaking was 'the ultimate nerdsnipe' for him; very optimistic about EigenDA and prolific AVS deployment as a security primitive other projects (e.g., T1) build on.
items.age_note: Stated Feb 2024 (protocol/network enthusiasm, not a price call); reinforced via T1 Nov 2024. Persisted.

citations.quote: Re-staking was the ultimate nerdsnipe for me, and I am very optimistic for the future presented by EigenDA and prolific AVS deployment.

items.rationale: Praised Morpho Blue as a 'big step for DeFi' and an a16z portfolio bet he continued backing into 2025.
items.age_note: Jan & Aug 2024, reaffirmed Jan 2025; enthusiasm for the network, with standing 'not investment advice' disclaimers.

citations.quote: Congrats to Paul Frambot and the whole Morpho team for launching Morpho Blue. One small step for Morpho, one big step for DeFi

items.rationale: Cited as a leading non-native DA layer central to his thesis that Ethereum needs modular DA to scale.
items.age_note: 2024 DA threads; not restated in 2025. Persisted, enthusiasm for the network rather than a token price view.

citations.quote: Enter non-native data availability like eigen_da, EspressoSys, CelestiaOrg.

risk_regime.rationale: Relentlessly, explicitly optimistic ('be fervently and definitely optimistic'). His most recent (2025) commentary points to regulatory wins, institutional adoption, record stablecoin volume and a coming 'sales era of crypto', and pushes builders not to give up — a clear pro-risk, builder-bullish posture. Caveat: optimism is paired with a demand for real PMF/value over hype, so it is conviction in fundamentals rather than indiscriminate speculation.

notes: Guy is a builder/early-stage investor (a16z crypto, CSX accelerator), so his 'views' are sector and protocol theses, not liquid-token trades; nearly every post carries a 'not investment advice' disclaimer, so tokens_excited reflects protocol/network enthusiasm, not price calls, and tokens_concerned is empty (no bearish token statements). His through-line and highest conviction is DePIN (energy, telecom, transportation, plus AI/healthcare/robotics). A genuine emphasis shift over the corpus: 2024 centered on ZK/proving/DA/rollup-scaling infrastructure, while 2025 tilts toward privacy/client-side proving (Miden), crypto x AI (Ambient, verifiable inference), payments/stablecoins/micropayments, and a 'sales era' framing — an evolution, not a reversal of earlier views. No hard reversals detected; older infra theses (DA, DeFi ZK coprocessors) are marked persisted where not restated in 2025. He is consistently dismissive of token-first/narrative-first thinking ('the token is not the product', 'vibes not value'), which tempers the otherwise strongly risk-on posture.
